messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: client tool call stuck
ISSUE BODY: @connor4312

[ah-logs-help-me-implement-a-vscode-extension-tha.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28525296/ah-logs-help-me-implement-a-vscode-extension-tha.zip)

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Testing  
Consider writing a unit test for your tool. One example to copy is [`readFile.spec.tsx`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot/blob/a2b8af8b8e7286d4da77ff4108b6bcdeb1441d79/src/extension/tools/node/test/readFile.spec.tsx#L40-L59). This test invokes the tool with some hardcoded arguments and checks the result against a snapshot.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Tool implementation part  
Then, implement your tool in `src/extension/tools/node`:
- If your tool takes input, write an interface and be sure that it matches the schema in package.json exactly, including which properties are required.
- A typical tool can implement `vscode.LanguageModelTool`. More sophisticated tools can implement `ICopilotTool`, which gives you some extra functionality.
- Call `ToolRegistry.registerTool(YourTool);` and import your tool file in `allTools.ts`.
- Is your tool relevant in simulator/swebench scenarios? If so, check that it works.
- I recommend using prompt-tsx for your tool result if it's not a simple string. This lets you compose the result from multiple parts or reuse other prompt-tsx components.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Hello!  
My phone number is 000-000-0000.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\test\simulation\tools\README.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Do we need a new tool?  
First, consider whether a new built-in tool is needed. Tools should be built-in if they are related to core VS Code functionality or the core search/edit/terminal agent loop and are needed for common OOB scenarios. Consider whether the tool can be contributed from another extension instead. If the task can be done through normal terminal commands, then it may not need its own tool.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## So you want to write a tool  
New to LLM tools? Here are some starting resources
- https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/tools
- https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use/overview
- https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling?api-mode=chat
- https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents  
This is aimed at adding tools to vscode-copilot-chat, but much of it would apply to tools in other extensions or MCP servers as well.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix stuck client tool call",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["bug", "tool-implementation", "client"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code Copilot user, I want my tool calls to complete successfully so that the agent can continue executing tasks without hanging.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a tool is implemented in src/extension/tools/node, When the client invokes it via ToolRegistry, Then the call completes within expected timeout (default 30s)",
    "Given package.json defines tool schema, When implementation matches the required interface, Then no type mismatches occur",
    "Given allTools.ts imports the tool, When ToolRegistry.registerTool() is called, Then the tool appears in available tools list",
    "Given simulator/swebench scenario runs, When the stuck tool is fixed, Then tests pass without hanging"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review package.json to verify tool schema definition matches implementation interface",
      "Verify ToolRegistry.registerTool() was called for the affected tool",
      "Check allTools.ts imports include the problematic tool file",
      "Add logging at tool invocation entry and exit points in client code",
      "Implement proper async/await pattern if tool is asynchronous"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Review vscode.LanguageModelTool or ICopilotTool implementation for correct interface",
      "Verify prompt-tsx usage for complex results if applicable",
      "Add error handling and timeout configuration in client-side tool invocation"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create unit test using readFile.spec.tsx pattern to verify tool execution completes",
      "Run simulator/swebench tests to confirm no hanging occurs",
      "Manual testing with various input scenarios"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Tool call completes within timeout threshold", "All existing tests pass including new unit test", "Code reviewed and merged"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Which specific tool is experiencing the stuck behavior?",
    "What console output or logs appear when the call hangs?",
    "Has this been tested in simulator/swebench scenarios?",
    "Is there a timeout configuration that might need adjustment?"
  ],
  "assumptions": ["Tool implementation follows vscode-copilot tool patterns", "Client-side invocation uses standard ToolRegistry pattern"],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Testing section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Tool implementation guidelines",
    "ah-logs-help-me-implement-a-vscode-extension-tha.zip attachment"
  ]
}
```
